title: Cyanea eleeleensis

intro: Cyanea eleeleensis, also known as the Ma'o hau hele, is a rare and endangered Hawaiian flowering plant that belongs to the family Campanulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and cold. The bioactive constituents of Cyanea eleeleensis include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is made into an herbal tea or a topical ointment, and it is also used in some modern herbal preparations, such as capsules and tinctures.
